title: Quinoa Vegetable Khichdi

subs.name: broken wheat (dalia) — cheaper and widely available; same cooking method
masoor dal (red lentils) — no soaking needed, cooks in the same time

ingredients.name: cooking oil (or ghee for a richer taste)

subs.name: ghee — adds richness but contains dairy (not vegan)

steps.text: Rinse the quinoa through a fine mesh strainer under running water for 1 minute — this removes the bitter saponin coating. Rinse the moong dal once. Soak both together in water for 10 minutes while you prep the vegetables.
Heat ghee or oil in a heavy-bottomed pot on medium. Add cumin seeds and asafoetida; let the seeds sputter for 20 seconds. Add ginger and fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the diced carrot and peas, stir for 1 minute. Add turmeric and toss briefly. Drain the quinoa and dal and add them to the pot, stirring to coat in the tempered oil.

Pour in 2.5 cups of water and salt. Bring to a full boil on high, then reduce the flame to low, cover tightly and cook for 15-17 minutes until the quinoa grains have unfurled their white tails and the dal is fully soft. Stir once halfway through — add a splash of water if it looks too dry.

Add the torn spinach, stir in and cook covered for 1 more minute until wilted. Remove from heat, squeeze lemon juice over the top and garnish with coriander. Serve hot — khichdi thickens as it sits, so eat promptly or add a little hot water when reheating.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.

substitution_notes: quinoa → broken wheat (dalia) — cheaper and widely available; same cooking method; oil → ghee — adds richness but contains dairy (not vegan); moong-dal → masoor dal (red lentils) — no soaking needed, cooks in the same time
